root_cause.text: The SXSSF cell implementation incorrectly overwrote the formula cell type with a string cell type when evaluating and setting rich text string values, resulting in the loss of the original formula.
root_cause.evidence: The patch modifies `ensureRichTextStringType()` in `SXSSFCell.java` to explicitly check `if(_value.getType() == CellType.FORMULA)` and instantiate a `RichTextStringFormulaValue` to preserve the formula type, with a comment stating `// don't change cell type for formulas`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The SXSSF cell implementation incorrectly overwrote the formula cell type with a string cell type when evaluating and setting rich text string values, resulting in the loss of the original formula.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch modifies `ensureRichTextStringType()` in `SXSSFCell.java` to explicitly check `if(_value.getType() == CellType.FORMULA)` and instantiate a `RichTextStringFormulaValue` to preserve the formula type, with a comment stating `// don't change cell type for formulas`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused logger instance from SXSSFCell.
intent_record.what: Removed the static POILogger declaration from the SXSSFCell class.
intent_record.how: Eliminates the initialization of an unused logger instance, slightly reducing memory and initialization overhead.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix ClassCastException by handling formula value types correctly when setting rich text string cell values.
intent_record.what: Added an instance check for RichTextStringFormulaValue before setting the cell value.
intent_record.how: Prevents ClassCastExceptions by applying the correct cast and setter method depending on whether the internal value represents a formula or standard rich text.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix ClassCastException by adding explicit handling for RichTextStringFormulaValue in formula evaluations.
intent_record.what: Added an else-if branch to check for RichTextStringFormulaValue and set its pre-evaluated value as an XSSFRichTextString.
intent_record.how: Prevents a ClassCastException by correctly routing RichTextStringFormulaValue instances to their appropriate setter instead of defaulting to StringFormulaValue.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix ClassCastException by adding explicit support for extracting pre-evaluated values from RichTextStringFormulaValue.
intent_record.what: Added an instance check to distinguish between RichTextStringFormulaValue and StringFormulaValue.
intent_record.how: Prevents ClassCastExceptions by applying the correct cast and extraction method based on the specific type of the formula value.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix loss of formula data when enforcing rich text string type on a formula cell.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check if the cell type is a formula, creating a RichTextStringFormulaValue to preserve the formula string, while retaining the existing logic for non-formula types.
intent_record.how: Prevents formulas from being overwritten with empty rich text strings by explicitly handling the formula cell type and transferring its existing value to the new rich text formula object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add RichTextStringFormulaValue class to support rich text string formula values.
intent_record.what: Added a new static nested class RichTextStringFormulaValue extending FormulaValue.
intent_record.how: Provides a formula value representation for rich text strings by storing a RichTextString pre-evaluated value.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix formula cell serialization by explicitly writing the cached result type attribute.
intent_record.what: Added a nested switch statement to explicitly write the cell type attribute based on the cached formula result type.
intent_record.how: Ensures the generated XML correctly specifies the data type of cached formula results, preventing misinterpretation by spreadsheet readers.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add missing serialization handlers for string, boolean, and error cell types.
intent_record.what: Replaced the default switch case with explicit handlers for STRING, BOOLEAN, and ERROR types.
intent_record.how: Adds specific serialization logic for these cell types, allowing them to be correctly written to the output stream.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import for JUnit assertFalse to support test assertions.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for JUnit's assertFalse method.
intent_record.how: Makes the assertFalse method available for use within the test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor test assertions to use the idiomatic assertFalse method.
intent_record.what: Replaced assertEquals(false, ...) with assertFalse(...).
intent_record.how: Utilizes a more idiomatic testing method without changing the underlying test behavior or logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused IOException declaration and suppress constant condition IDE warnings in the test method.
intent_record.what: Removed the `throws IOException` declaration from the test method and added a `//noinspection ConstantConditions` comment.
intent_record.how: Cleans up the method signature by removing an unnecessary exception declaration and suppresses IDE warnings regarding constant conditions in the test.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update font index retrieval to use getFontIndexAsInt() for API compatibility.
intent_record.what: Changed the method call from getFontIndex() to getFontIndexAsInt() when retrieving the font index from the cell style.
intent_record.how: Updates the API usage to fetch the font index as an integer, ensuring compatibility with updated library versions where the older method is deprecated or changed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertions to use idiomatic assertTrue and assertFalse.
intent_record.what: Replaced assertEquals with boolean literals with assertTrue and assertFalse.
intent_record.how: Improves code readability and uses more idiomatic JUnit assertions without changing the test logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertions to use more idiomatic assertTrue and assertFalse methods.
intent_record.what: Replaced assertEquals calls containing boolean literals with the corresponding assertTrue and assertFalse methods.
intent_record.how: Improves test code readability and uses more direct assertion methods without altering the underlying test execution or behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace assertTrue with assertEquals for better test failure reporting.
intent_record.what: Changed a test assertion from assertTrue with equals to assertEquals.
intent_record.how: Improves the test failure message by directly using assertEquals instead of assertTrue with a boolean condition, without changing the test's passing logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add assertions to verify cell types and formulas are preserved before and after evaluation in the test case.
intent_record.what: Added assertions to check cell types and cell formulas for three different cells before and after formula evaluation.
intent_record.how: Enhances the test suite by ensuring that reading and evaluating formula cells preserves their correct cell type and formula definition without modification.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary java.util imports for date and time handling.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for Date, GregorianCalendar, Locale, and TimeZone from the java.util package.
intent_record.how: Makes these classes available for use within the file, likely to support date and time manipulation in upcoming code.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertions to use idiomatic assertTrue and assertFalse.
intent_record.what: Changed assertEquals with boolean literals to assertFalse and assertTrue.
intent_record.how: Improves test readability and uses more idiomatic assertion methods without altering test behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertions to use the more appropriate assertTrue and assertFalse methods.

intent_record.how: Improves the clarity and standard usage of assertions in the test cases without changing the test logic or outcomes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace boolean assertEquals with assertTrue and assertFalse for better idiomatic testing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor Calendar.set arguments to use explicit constants and remove leading zeros for better readability.
intent_record.what: Replaced the numeric month argument with a Calendar constant and removed leading zeros from integer parameters.
intent_record.how: Improves code readability by making the month explicitly clear, mitigating common off-by-one errors associated with Java's zero-indexed Calendar months.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertion to use the more idiomatic assertTrue method.

intent_record.how: Simplifies the test assertion without altering the test logic or behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean test assertion to use assertFalse for improved code readability.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor boolean assertion to use the more idiomatic assertTrue method.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test assertions to verify cell type consistency when setting a value on a formula cell.
intent_record.what: Added two assertions to check the cell type.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the cell type remains FORMULA after setting a rich text value in the test.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor test assertions to use updated API methods and improve readability.

intent_record.how: Introduces test coverage to ensure that calling 'setCellValue' with various data types on a formula cell does not alter its underlying CellType.FORMULA status.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests to verify the correct evaluation of SUM and VLOOKUP formulas.
intent_record.what: Added two new test methods, testFormulaEvaluatorEvaluateSimpleFormulaCell and testFormulaEvaluatorEvaluateVlookupFormulaCell, to BaseTestFormulaEvaluator.
intent_record.how: Increases test coverage by verifying that the FormulaEvaluator correctly evaluates both simple SUM formulas and more complex VLOOKUP formulas involving hidden sheets and data validations.

grouping_result.intent: Remove unused logger instance from SXSSFCell.
Fix loss of formula data when enforcing rich text string type on a formula cell.
Add RichTextStringFormulaValue class to support rich text string formula values.
Fix formula cell serialization by explicitly writing the cached result type attribute.
Add missing serialization handlers for string, boolean, and error cell types.
Add a test case to verify that setting cell values preserves the formula cell type.
Refactor test assertions to use updated API methods and improve readability.
Remove unused IOException declaration and suppress constant condition IDE warnings in the test method.
Update font index retrieval to use the integer-based method getFontIndexAsInt().
Replace assertTrue with assertEquals for better test failure reporting.
Add assertions to verify cell types and formulas are preserved before and after evaluation in the test case.
Add necessary java.util imports for date and time handling.
Refactor Calendar.set arguments to use explicit constants and remove leading zeros for better readability.
Add necessary imports to support test functionality.
Add unit tests to verify the correct evaluation of SUM and VLOOKUP formulas.

consistency_review.core_intent: Fix issues and ClassCastExceptions related to handling rich text strings in formula cells.
Add test cases and assertions to verify that setting cell values preserves the formula cell type.
Refactor boolean test assertions to use the more idiomatic assertTrue and assertFalse methods.
Update font index retrieval to use the integer-based method getFontIndexAsInt()

relevance_review.reason: Removing an unused logger is a code cleanup task and does not address the logic issue of incorrectly overwriting the formula cell type.

The group's changes directly address the issue of formula cells incorrectly changing type and losing formula data when setting rich text string values, which perfectly matches the vulnerability root cause.

The addition of the RichTextStringFormulaValue class directly addresses the root cause by providing proper support for rich text string values in formula cells, preventing the incorrect overwriting of the cell type.

The group addresses formula cell serialization and explicitly writing the cached result type, which directly relates to fixing how formula results (such as evaluated string values) are handled and stored without losing the original formula type.
The group adds missing serialization handlers for various cell types, which is unrelated to the vulnerability involving the incorrect overwriting of the formula cell type during rich text evaluation.
The group adds test cases and assertions explicitly designed to verify that setting cell values preserves the formula cell type, which directly targets the described vulnerability root cause.
The group consists entirely of test refactoring to use idiomatic boolean assertions (assertTrue/assertFalse) and does not address the issue of formula cell types being incorrectly overwritten.
The group only contains refactoring and cleanup in a test method (removing unused exceptions and suppressing warnings), which is unrelated to fixing the formula cell type overwrite vulnerability.
The group updates font index retrieval to use an integer-based method, which is completely unrelated to the vulnerability involving cell types and formula overwrites.
The group's changes are purely for improving test assertions (replacing assertTrue with assertEquals) and do not address the vulnerability's root cause regarding cell type overwriting.

The group adds test assertions specifically to verify that cell types and formulas are correctly preserved, which directly tests the fix for the vulnerability where formulas were incorrectly overwritten.
Adding imports for date and time handling is unrelated to fixing the issue where formula cell types were incorrectly overwritten with string cell types.
The group's changes consist of refactoring Calendar.set arguments for readability, which has no connection to the SXSSF cell formula type overwriting issue described in the root cause.
The group only adds imports to support test functionality and does not contain the actual fix for the SXSSF cell formula type overwriting issue.

The group adds general unit tests for SUM and VLOOKUP formulas, which does not directly address or test the specific root cause of SXSSF cells incorrectly overwriting the formula cell type with a string cell type when handling rich text strings.

candidates.rationale: This statement replaces the existing cell value with a new RichTextValue, overwriting any existing formula data when attempting to set a rich text string.
This function call leads to the cell type being enforced as a rich text string, causing the loss of the original formula if the cell was a formula cell.

vuln_statements.explanation: This function call incorrectly enforces the cell type to be a rich text string, causing the loss of the original formula if the cell was a formula cell.
This statement replaces the existing cell value with a new RichTextValue, overwriting any existing formula data.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `setCellValue(RichTextString)` contains the vulnerable construct `ensureRichTextStringType()`, which blindly overwrites the cell type with a string type without checking if the cell is currently a formula. The commit only adds null checking but does not fix the issue of preserving formula cell types.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `setCellValue(RichTextString value)` calls `ensureRichTextStringType()` which unconditionally sets the type to string, overwriting any existing formula type. It lacks the correct handling seen in `setCellValue(String value)` which uses `ensureTypeOrFormulaType(CELL_TYPE_STRING)`.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Bugzilla 51160: Initial version of SXSSF, a low memory foortprint API to produce xlsx files

git-svn-id: https://svn.apa
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 50a40785edac18968aa83e50297ee53896aed241
Author: Yegor Kozlov <yegor@apache.org>
Date:   Tue May 17 10:46:35 2011 +0000

    Bugzilla 51160: Initial version of SXSSF, a low memory foortprint API to produce xlsx files
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/poi/trunk@1104120 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/src/ooxml/java/org/apache/poi/xssf/streaming/SXSSFCell.java b/src/ooxml/java/org/apache/poi/xssf/streaming/SXSSFCell.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000
bic_trace_step.reason: In `setCellValue(RichTextString value)`, the function calls `ensureRichTextStringType()`, which unconditionally replaces the cell's `_value` with a new `RichTextValue` (a string type) if the cell is not currently a rich text string type. Unlike `setCellValue(String value)` which uses `ensureTypeOrFormulaType()` and preserves formula cells by calling `setPreEvaluatedValue()`, the rich text handling incorrectly overwrites formula cells, causing the loss of the original formula.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: While working on bug 61148 I found that getCellType() and setCellErrorValue() in SXSSFCell are doing different things th
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 6bdce2bfd7732a63cd0e9f7b7d9816e5f6f0fe86
Author: Dominik Stadler <centic@apache.org>
Date:   Tue Sep 19 12:47:13 2017 +0000

    While working on bug 61148 I found that getCellType() and setCellErrorValue() in SXSSFCell are doing different things than the ones in XSSFCell, tried to fix this by mimicking functionality of XSSF in SXSSF as well.
    Reformating some small things as well.
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/poi/trunk@1808874 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450ede
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `ensureRichTextStringType()` contains the vulnerable logic where it blindly overwrites `_value` with a new `RichTextValue()` if the type is not `CellType.STRING` or is not rich text (`if(_value.getType()!=CellType.STRING ||!((StringValue)_value).isRichText()) _value = new RichTextValue();`). This causes formula cells to lose their original formula when setting rich text string values. The commit only applies formatting changes to this function and does not fix the root cause.

Author: Yegor Kozlov <yegor@apache.org>

    Bugzilla 51160: Initial version of SXSSF, a low memory foortprint API to produce xlsx files

new file mode 100644
index 0000
bic_trace_step.reason: The method `setCellValue(RichTextString value)` calls `ensureRichTextStringType()`, which unconditionally replaces `_value` with a new `RichTextValue` (a string cell type) if it's not already a rich text string type. This means if the cell currently contains a formula, the formula is lost and overwritten, matching the vulnerability description.
